name: Credit Agreement Summary
description: Produces a structured legal summary of a credit agreement — parties and lender group, facilities, key economic terms, covenants, events of default, security, and unusual or off-market terms — with clause and schedule citations throughout, delivered as a Word document.
promptTemplate:
Prepare a structured legal summary of the following credit agreement.

Matter: {{description}}

Documents provided: {{document}}

Cover, in order:
1. Parties — borrower(s), guarantors/obligors, agent(s), security trustee/agent, and the lender group, with their roles.
2. Facilities — each facility's type (term, revolving, swingline, etc.), commitment amount and currency, purpose, availability period, and maturity/termination date.
3. Key economic terms — margin and any ratchet, reference rate and fallbacks, fees (commitment, arrangement, agency, prepayment), default interest, and mandatory/voluntary prepayment mechanics.
4. Covenants — financial covenants with their definitions, ratios and testing dates; the material affirmative and negative covenants (negative pledge, disposals, financial indebtedness, acquisitions, distributions); and any baskets or carve-outs of note.
5. Events of default — each EoD with its grace period, threshold and cure rights, plus cross-default/cross-acceleration scope.
6. Security and guarantees — the security package, guarantee structure, and any agreed security principles or release mechanics.
7. Unusual or non-market terms — anything one-sided, missing, or out of step with market standard for a facility of this kind, with a short explanation of why it matters.

Cite the clause, schedule, or paragraph reference for every point summarised. Deliver the summary using the docx_generate tool as a Word document, one section per topic above, and report the output file path.
